primary_accounting_issue: Déterminer si le revenu issu d’une licence de propriété intellectuelle est comptabilisé selon un modèle de droit d’accès ou de droit d’utilisation, ce qui détermine une reconnaissance étalée ou à une date donnée.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule by selecting the IFRS standard that directly governs revenue from licences of intellectual property, and there were no overlapping lower-precedence governing documents in the provided context to discard.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty because IFRS 15 contains specific licence guidance that directly addresses whether revenue is recognised over time or at a point in time.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern revenue recognition for licences of intellectual property and define the two core licence models.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs provide the underlying revenue recognition framework for over-time versus point-in-time recognition referenced by the licence guidance.
These paragraphs support the measurement of progress only after the over-time licence model has been identified.
These paragraphs clarify when the entity’s activities significantly affect the intellectual property for the right-to-access model.
This paragraph constrains the licence assessment by excluding certain factors from distinguishing the two licence models.
These paragraphs provide a royalty-specific overlay relevant to licence revenue but do not create a separate top-level model for the issue asked.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs address broader revenue topics such as general control, real estate, principal versus agent, financing, and variable consideration that are not needed to identify the peer licence models.

treatment_families.family: Distinct licence of intellectual property classified by the nature of the promise
Licence combined with other goods or services as a single performance obligation

approaches.label: Right to access intellectual property
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 15 identifies this as a distinct licence model when the customer receives access to intellectual property as it exists throughout the licence period.

approaches.label: Right to use intellectual property
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 15 identifies this as a distinct licence model when the customer receives a right to use the intellectual property as it exists when the licence is granted.
